value.found_quote: MCP servers communicate with your agent using different transport protocols... If the docs give you a command to run (like `npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-github`), use stdio... If the docs give you a URL, use HTTP or SSE... If you're building your own tools in code, use an SDK MCP server
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes three transport types for MCP servers: (1) stdio — local processes communicating via stdin/stdout, (2) HTTP/SSE — for cloud-hosted MCP servers and remote APIs, and (3) SDK MCP servers — custom tools defined directly in application code (SDK-embedded). This directly substantiates all parts of the claim: stdio for local processes, HTTP/SSE for cloud-hosted or remote APIs, and SDK-embedded transports. The page presents these as active, generally available features with working code examples.
